# Capabilities

OmniMesh ships with every QVAC capability wired through the mesh.
The list below is generated from the protocol types in
`packages/protocol/src/capabilities.ts` — see
[`gen-capabilities.ts`](https://github.com/omnimesh/omni/blob/main/docs/scripts/gen-capabilities.ts).

<!-- The matrix below is regenerated by docs/scripts/gen-capabilities.ts. Do not hand-edit. -->
<!-- BEGIN CAPABILITIES MATRIX -->
| Capability | Host | Worker | Mobile | OpenAI |
| ---------- | :--: | :----: | :----: | :----: |
| LLM chat | yes | yes | yes | yes |
| Embeddings | yes | yes | yes | no |
| Retrieval-augmented generation | yes | yes | no | no |
| Fine-tuning | yes | yes | no | no |
| Multimodal | yes | yes | yes | yes |
| Image generation | yes | yes | no | no |
| Video generation | yes | yes | no | no |
| ASR (speech → text) | yes | yes | yes | no |
| TTS (text → speech) | yes | yes | yes | no |
| Voice assistant (oww → asr → llm → tts) | no | yes | yes | no |
| Translation | yes | yes | yes | no |
| Vision-language action | yes | yes | no | no |
| OCR | yes | yes | yes | no |
| Image classification | yes | yes | yes | no |
| P2P delegated inference | yes | yes | yes | no |
| Distributed model registry | yes | yes | yes | no |
| Model asset streaming | yes | yes | yes | no |
| Blind relays for NAT | yes | yes | yes | no |
<!-- END CAPABILITIES MATRIX -->

The `Host` and `Worker` columns are gated on the QVAC SDK being installed
(`omni install qvac`); without it, both still run in
relay-only / dashboard mode.

## System Dependencies

The QVAC capabilities above ride on five installable system
dependencies. Run `omni install <capability>` on any host or
worker, or use the **INSTALL** tab in the dashboard to dispatch
to connected peers.

| Capability     | Description            | darwin                   | linux                                                | win32              | Restart |
| -------------- | ---------------------- | ------------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------- | ------------------ | :-----: |
| `qvac`         | `@qvac/sdk` (provider) | `bun add @qvac/sdk`      | `bun add @qvac/sdk`                                  | `bun add @qvac/sdk` |  yes   |
| `ffmpeg`       | Media toolchain        | `brew install ffmpeg`    | `sudo apt-get install -y ffmpeg`                     | `winget install ffmpeg` | no |
| `vulkan`       | GPU drivers + tools    | n/a (Metal)              | `sudo apt-get install -y mesa-vulkan-drivers vulkan-tools` | vendor drivers | no |
| `openwakeword` | Wake-word model cache  | writes `~/.cache/omni/oww/.installed`                | same                                                 | same               | no      |
| `bonjour`      | mDNS discovery         | `bun add bonjour-service`| `bun add bonjour-service`                            | `bun add bonjour-service` | no |

All five are idempotent — running `omni install <cap>` a second
time is a no-op. Only `qvac` requires a host/worker restart
after install (auto-restart is wired in).

### Usage

```bash
# Local
omni install qvac --yes
omni install --list                # show available capabilities
omni install ffmpeg --dry-run      # show the command without running

# Remote (via dashboard)
# Open the INSTALL tab → click INSTALL next to the row × peer you want.
# Progress streams live; cancel button is enabled while running.
```

### Doctor + --fix

`omni doctor` surfaces every missing capability (above) and
every static check (bun, node, platform, secret, host http,
openai http, git sha). Pass `--fix` to auto-install the
missing capabilities in one pass:

```bash
omni doctor           # report only
omni doctor --fix     # report, then install missing capabilities
```

Add a new installable capability by dropping a file into
`packages/installer/src/capabilities/`; the registry picks it
up automatically.
